notes: Round-3 LLM-as-judge per worked-example §3.5: literal implementation of the JUDGE template + next-token logits read of yes/no. Uses Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct (the smallest Qwen2.5 instruct model, ~500M params, ~1 GB in bf16) so the per-round compute budget on Codabench T4 is comfortable; the §3.5 spec itself notes a smaller backbone is the standard way to fit per-tier budget. Moves the F5 cell of tab:coverage from × to ✓. Zero-shot only: labeled[] is unused in this baseline (the spec's 'stronger variant' is in-context calibration via labeled[] few-shot examples; we deliberately ship the zero-shot variant first as the literal §3.5 NCF-equivalent of sub 33).

hyperparams.decoding: read next-token log-prob of YES_ID and NO_ID, renormalize over {yes,no}
